lecture_context.lecture_title: Stanford CS231N Lecture 2: Image Classification with Linear Classifiers

unit.name: k-NN decision regions and L1 versus L2 distance
unit.description: Explains how 1-NN and k-NN partition feature space, why outliers and ties matter, and how L1 and L2 distance induce different geometries and decision boundaries.
unit.summary: The lecture visualizes 1-nearest-neighbor as a partition of feature space in which each region inherits the label of the closest sample [ts=1571s][ts=1608s][ts=1617s]. A single outlier can create a large erroneous region, motivating k-nearest-neighbor with majority voting over multiple neighbors for robustness [ts=1632s][ts=1652s][ts=1664s][ts=1680s]. This introduces ambiguity regions where ties prevent a confident decision; the instructor points out that such regions are useful signals for where additional data should be collected [ts=1696s][ts=1716s][ts=1732s]. The segment then compares L1 and L2 distances geometrically: L1 has diamond-like equal-distance contours aligned with feature axes [ts=1767s][ts=1799s], while L2 has circular contours from squaring and square-rooting differences [ts=1822s][ts=1839s][ts=1849s]. The lecture uses rotation sensitivity to explain when L1 preserves meaningful feature-axis structure and why L2 yields smoother, more rotation-invariant boundaries [ts=1893s][ts=1906s][ts=2001s][ts=2073s].

key_points.text: 1-nearest-neighbor partitions space by assigning each region to the closest labeled sample.
A single outlier can create a disproportionately large region under 1-NN.
k-NN predicts by majority vote over the K closest neighbors.
Ambiguous tie regions indicate places where collecting more data would help.
L1 distance yields axis-aligned diamond-like equal-distance contours; L2 yields circular contours.

kp_catalog.name: Nearest neighbor classification
kp_catalog.description: A nonparametric classifier that stores all labeled examples and predicts by copying the label of the closest stored example.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because nearest neighbor classification clarifies a nonparametric classifier that stores all labeled examples and predicts by copying the label of the closest stored example, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Distance metrics for similarity
kp_catalog.description: The choice of L1 or L2 distance changes which examples count as nearest and changes the geometry of decision regions.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because distance metrics for similarity clarifies the choice of L1 or L2 distance changes which examples count as nearest and changes the geometry of decision regions, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.
